factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is occurring, particularly in the Accelera segment, as part of cost reduction efforts.

evidence.text: In the fourth quarter of 2024, our Accelera segment underwent a strategic review... As a result of these actions, we recorded several charges in the fourth quarter related to inventory write-downs, intangible and fixed asset impairments and joint venture impairments. Total charges for these strategic reorganization actions were $312 million.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages are being strengthened through focus on electrified power systems and hydrogen production technologies.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products and improvements of current technologies to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas-powered engines and related components, as well as development activities around electrified power systems with innovative components and systems including battery and electric power technologies and hydrogen production technologies.

factors.summary: Labor costs are creating margin pressure, primarily due to higher compensation expenses across multiple segments.

evidence.text: Engine segment EBITDA increased $23 million, primarily due to favorable pricing, partially offset by lower volumes, increased product coverage, higher compensation expenses, higher supply chain related costs and lower joint venture technology fees. [...] Distribution segment EBITDA increased $169 million, primarily due to favorable pricing, partially offset by higher compensation expenses.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance with a slight increase in the expected effective tax rate for 2025, signaling cautious optimism.

evidence.text: Our effective tax rate for 2025 is expected to approximate 24.5 percent, excluding any discrete tax items that may arise.

factors.summary: New product launches and contract decisions, particularly in power generation and data center markets, are expected to drive future growth.

evidence.text: We expect demand within our Power Systems business to remain strong, including the power generation and mining markets. [...] North American sales increased $544 million principally due to higher demand in power generation markets, especially data center and commercial markets.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are benefiting the company with lower borrowing costs, though higher rates could impact investment and consumer spending.

evidence.text: The company is benefiting from lower interest rates, as seen in the refinancing at lower rates.
evidence.subsection: Key Working Capital and Balance Sheet Data

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are creating both favorable and unfavorable impacts, with gains in certain currencies offsetting losses in others.

evidence.text: The foreign currency translation adjustment was a net loss of $276 million and net gain of $92 million for the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023, respectively.
evidence.subsection: 2023 vs. 2022 / Comprehensive Income - Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment

factors.summary: Supply chain health is a concern, with disruptions and longer lead times affecting certain segments, though the company is working to improve operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Higher supply chain related costs [...]

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are under scrutiny, with incidents and recalls potentially impacting the company's reputation and costs.

evidence.text: Included a $28 million impairment of our joint venture with KAMAZ and $3 million of royalty charges as part of our costs associated with the indefinite suspension of our Russian operations.

factors.summary: The company is adopting digital technologies and automation, particularly in its Components segment, to enhance operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Beginning in the second quarter of 2024, we realigned certain businesses within our Components segment to be consistent with how our segment leader now monitors performance. [...] The change had no impact on our consolidated results.

factors.summary: New products, such as electrolyzers and advanced power systems, are gaining traction, contributing to revenue growth in higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Accelera segment sales increased $60 million mainly due to improved sales of electrolyzers, partially offset by lower electrified powertrain sales.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs are creating challenges, particularly in the Components segment, where sales decreased due to divestitures and lower demand in China.

evidence.text: Sales decreased $1.3 billion due to the Atmus divestiture on March 18, 2024. [...] Emission solutions sales decreased $234 million principally due to lower demand in China.

factors.summary: Environmental and social commitments are creating costs and risks, particularly in the context of sustainable technologies and compliance.

evidence.text: Included a $28 million impairment of our joint venture with KAMAZ and $3 million of royalty charges as part of our costs associated with the indefinite suspension of our Russian operations.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are mixed, with expectations of improvement in North American pick-up truck demand but weakness in medium-duty and heavy-duty truck markets.

evidence.text: We expect demand for medium-duty and heavy-duty trucks in North America to remain relatively weak in the first half of 2025. [...] We expect North American pick-up truck demand to improve.

factors.summary: Dealer inventory levels are healthy, with orders indicating growing demand in certain segments like power generation and data centers.

evidence.text: Sales for our Distribution segment by region, were as follows: [...] North America $ 7,625 [...] due to higher demand in power generation markets, especially data center and commercial markets.

factors.summary: Aftermarket service revenue is growing faster than new equipment sales, driven by strong demand in Engine and Power Systems businesses.

evidence.text: We anticipate demand in our aftermarket business will continue to be robust, driven primarily by strong demand in our Engine and Power Systems businesses.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed. While gross and operating margins have shown improvement in certain areas due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, they are under pressure in others due to higher compensation expenses, supply chain costs, and the divestiture of Atmus.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $190 million and increased 0.5 points as a percentage of sales. The increases were mainly due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, partially offset by the divestiture of Atmus, higher compensation expenses and increased product coverage.

Engine segment EBITDA increased $23 million, primarily due to favorable pricing, partially offset by lower volumes, increased product coverage, higher compensation expenses, higher supply chain related costs and lower joint venture technology fees.
Distribution segment EBITDA increased $169 million, primarily due to favorable pricing, partially offset by higher compensation expenses.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has shown improvement overall, driven by strong operating cash flows and effective liquidity management. However, there is a discrepancy in the reported figures, with one source indicating a decrease in operating cash flow and another showing an increase. This inconsistency suggests a need for further clarification on the actual performance.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operations is our principal source of liquidity with $1.5 billion provided in 2024. [...] Our financial condition and liquidity remain strong.
We generated $1.5 billion of operating cash flows in 2024, compared to $4.0 billion in 2023.

Net cash provided by operating activities $ 1,487   $ 3,966  [...] The effect of exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalents increased $28 million, primarily due to favorable fluctuations in the British pound, partially offset by the Brazilian real.

factors.summary: The company is improving its debt leverage through deleveraging efforts, which include issuing lower-cost debt and reducing total debt. However, there is a slight increase in debt balances, though the overall debt-to-capital ratio has improved.

evidence.text: In February, we issued $2.25 billion in long-term debt to pay down higher cost debt, finance the Settlement Agreements payments and improve our overall liquidity.
Total debt $ 7,059   $ 6,696  [...] Total debt as a percent of total capital 38.4   % 40.3  %

Our debt to capital ratio... was 38.4 percent, compared to 40.3 percent at December 31, 2023. The decrease was primarily due to the increased equity balance from stronger earnings since December 31, 2023, partially offset by higher debt balances at December 31, 2024.
evidence.subsection: Key Working Capital and Balance Sheet Data

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is mixed, with an expected increase in 2025 for growth but also a focus on managing expenditures strategically. While the majority of the capital expenditures are allocated to North America, there is no clear indication of how this aligns with the strategic investments mentioned elsewhere.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures are expected to increase in 2025, signaling confidence in future growth.
We plan to spend an estimated $1.4 billion to $1.5 billion in 2025 on capital expenditures with over 65 percent of these expenditures expected to be invested in North America.
Capital expenditures are being managed with a focus on strategic investments.
In July 2024, the Board of Directors (Board) authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of approximately 8 percent from $1.68 per share to $1.82 per share.
Our effective tax rate for 2025 is expected to approximate 24.5 percent, excluding any discrete tax items that may arise.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated a commitment to enhancing shareholder returns through dividend increases. However, there is a lack of information on other return strategies such as buybacks, suggesting a cautious approach to capital management.

evidence.text: In July 2024, the Board authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of approximately 8 percent from $1.68 per share to $1.82 per share.
In July 2024, the Board of Directors (Board) authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of approximately 8 percent from $1.68 per share to $1.82 per share.
We fund our working capital with cash from operations and short-term borrowings, including commercial paper, when necessary.

factors.summary: The company's M&A activity has shifted towards strategic joint ventures and divestitures, with decreased acquisition activity. While the joint venture indicates confidence in growth opportunities, the divestiture has improved the financial position by reducing shares and generating significant gains.

evidence.text: In September 2023, our Accelera business signed an agreement to form a joint venture [...] to accelerate and localize battery cell production and the battery supply chain in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: Amplify Cell Technologies LLC Joint Venture

evidence.text: On March 18, 2024, we completed the divestiture of our remaining 80.5 percent ownership of Atmus Filtration Technologies Inc. (Atmus) common stock through a tax-free split-off. The exchange resulted in a reduction of shares of our common stock outstanding by 5.6 million shares and a gain of approximately $1.3 billion.

Net cash used in investing activities increased $139 million, primarily due to higher investments in equity investees of $228 million and cash associated with the Atmus divestiture of $174 million, partially offset by lower acquisition activity of $234 million.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is a significant and costly constraint for the company, impacting both operations and finances. The company has faced substantial expenses, including a $2.0 billion settlement related to emissions violations, and ongoing costs in emission solutions which are driving higher expenses.

evidence.text: Those and related expenses and reputational damage could have a material adverse impact on our results of operations, financial condition and cash flows.

In December 2023, we announced that we reached an agreement in principle with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the California Air Resources Board (CARB), the Environmental and Natural Resources Division of the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) and the California Attorney General’s Office to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications in the U.S., which became final and effective in April 2024.
Research, development and engineering expenses 328 387 309 59 15 % (78) (25) %

factors.summary: The company is facing significant regulatory changes, particularly in emissions standards and environmental regulations, which are increasing compliance costs and creating operational constraints. These changes are being driven by agreements with regulatory agencies such as the EPA and CARB, leading to unfavorable financial adjustments and settlements.

evidence.text: Our engines are subject to extensive statutory and regulatory requirements governing emissions and noise, including standards imposed by the EPA, the EU, state regulatory agencies (such as the CARB) and other regulatory agencies around the world.

In December 2023, we announced that we reached an agreement in principle with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the California Air Resources Board (CARB), the Environmental and Natural Resources Division of the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) and the California Attorney General’s Office to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications in the U.S., which became final and effective in April 2024.

Included $52 million of favorable return to provision adjustments, $22 million of favorable share-based compensation tax benefits, $21 million of favorable adjustments related to audit settlements and $20 million of favorable adjustments from tax return amendments, partially offset by $50 million of unfavorable adjustments related to Accelera strategic reorganization actions and a net $6 million of other unfavorable adjustments.

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges with input costs, including cost increases due to raw material and transportation price fluctuations, as well as inflationary pressures. While the company is working to mitigate these impacts, there are concerns about potential effects on earnings and the need to seek alternative supply sources.

evidence.text: We continue to experience pockets of supply chain disruptions and related challenges throughout the supply chain. Any delay in our suppliers' deliveries may adversely affect our operations at multiple manufacturing locations, forcing us to seek alternative supply sources to avoid serious disruptions.
Increases in costs, tariffs, as well as other inflationary pressures, could negatively impact earnings.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation risks primarily related to emissions and regulatory issues. Litigation exposure has led to substantial financial charges, including a $2.0 billion settlement. There is also a potential for material financial or operational impacts from ongoing cases.

evidence.text: In connection with our announcement of our entry into the agreement in principle, we became subject to shareholder, consumer and third-party litigation regarding the matters covered by the Settlement Agreements, and we may become subject to additional litigation in connection with these matters.

In December 2023, we announced that we reached an agreement in principle with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the California Air Resources Board (CARB), the Environmental and Natural Resources Division of the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) and the California Attorney General’s Office to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications in the U.S., which became final and effective in April 2024.
Included $22 million of unfavorable adjustments for uncertain tax positions and $3 million of net unfavorable other discrete tax items.

factors.summary: Productivity is mixed due to supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, though operational efficiency improvements are being implemented through restructuring and realignment of businesses.

evidence.text: The labor market for skilled manufacturing remains tight, and our labor costs have increased as a result. Material, transportation, labor and other cost inflation has impacted and could continue to impact our results of operations, financial condition and cash flows.

Beginning in the second quarter of 2024, we realigned certain businesses within our Components segment to be consistent with how our segment leader now monitors performance. [...] The change had no impact on our consolidated results.

factors.summary: The company exhibits mixed pricing power performance. While they are successfully raising prices in certain segments, contributing to revenue growth despite lower volumes, they face challenges in fully passing on increased material costs to customers, which could negatively impact their results.

evidence.text: Engine segment EBITDA increased $23 million, primarily due to favorable pricing, partially offset by lower volumes, increased product coverage, higher compensation expenses, higher supply chain related costs and lower joint venture technology fees.
evidence.subsection: Our products are exposed to variability in material and commodity costs.

evidence.text: Distribution segment EBITDA increased $169 million, primarily due to favorable pricing, partially offset by higher compensation expenses.

While we customarily enter into financial transactions and contractual pricing adjustment provisions with our customers that attempt to address some of these risks, there can be no assurance that commodity price fluctuations will not adversely affect our results of operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: Economic conditions present mixed impacts on the company, with challenges such as inflation and potential recessions affecting profitability, while strong demand in certain markets like power generation and data centers offers some positive tailwinds.

evidence.text: Increased levels of inflation, fluctuating interest rates and concerns regarding a potential economic recession may result in increased operating costs and/or decreased levels of profitability.
The overall economic environment is a mixed bag, with strong demand in power generation and data center markets but potential weakness in truck demand.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is mixed, with some segments experiencing higher utilization while others face challenges due to weak demand and fluctuating demand and supply chain issues.

evidence.text: Our markets are cyclical in nature and we face periods when demand fluctuates significantly higher or lower than our normal operating levels, including variability driven by supply chain inconsistency.
Unit shipments by engine classification [...] Heavy-duty 132,900 [...] (9,000) (6) % [...] Power generation $ 3,985 [...].

factors.summary: Geopolitical factors, including trade tensions and sanctions, present significant risks to the company's supply chain, operations, profitability, and market position. These factors could lead to shifts in customer preferences, trade disruptions, and restrictions on product sales in certain countries.

evidence.text: Changes in laws, regulations and government policies on foreign trade and investment can affect the demand for our products and services, cause non-U.S. customers to shift preferences toward domestically manufactured or branded products and impact the competitive position of our products or prevent us from being able to sell products in certain countries.
The potential for trade disruption, including embargoes, sanctions and export controls could negatively impact earnings.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across segments, with positive momentum in power generation and mining markets, but weakness in medium-duty and heavy-duty truck markets. Overall demand appears stable, though it fluctuates with economic conditions and can be impacted by OEM inventory levels, production schedules, stoppages, and supply chain challenges.

evidence.text: Demand in these markets tends to fluctuate in response to overall economic conditions. Our sales may also be impacted by OEM inventory levels, production schedules, stoppages and supply chain challenges.
We expect demand within our Power Systems business to remain strong, including the power generation and mining markets.
We expect demand for medium-duty and heavy-duty trucks in North America to remain relatively weak in the first half of 2025.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments such as power generation, which is positive for overall profitability. However, this shift is accompanied by challenges in other areas, including lower demand in heavy-duty truck and pick-up truck markets, and weaker demand in global construction markets.

evidence.text: Higher demand in power generation markets (mostly data center markets) and higher demand in North American medium-duty truck and bus markets were offset by the divestiture of Atmus, lower emission solutions demand (mainly in China), lower demand in North American heavy-duty truck and pick-up truck markets and weaker demand in global construction markets.
Sales for our Power Systems segment by product line were as follows: [...] Power generation $ 3,985 [...]

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with challenges particularly in medium-duty and heavy-duty truck segments, though other areas like power generation and mining remain strong. Strategic reorganization actions partially offset this deceleration.

evidence.text: The increases in net income attributable to Cummins Inc. and earnings per diluted share were driven by the absence of the $2.0 billion charge related to the Settlement Agreements in 2023 and the $1.3 billion gain recognized on the divestiture of Atmus in 2024.

We expect demand for medium-duty and heavy-duty trucks in North America to remain relatively weak in the first half of 2025. [...] We expect demand within our Power Systems business to remain strong, including the power generation and mining markets.

factors.summary: Unit volume trends are mixed across different segments. While some segments like Distribution and Power Systems are experiencing growth, others such as Engine and Components are facing declines or flat performance.

evidence.text: Net sales increased $37 million, primarily driven by the following: Distribution segment sales increased 11 percent... Power Systems segment sales increased 13 percent... Engine segment sales were flat... These increases were partially offset by decreased Components segment sales of 13 percent mainly due to the divestiture of Atmus on March 18, 2024.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions, particularly in the Accelera segment, are delivering measurable savings. The total charges associated with these strategic reorganization actions amount to $312 million, which have positively impacted the segment's EBITDA.

evidence.text: Total charges for these strategic reorganization actions were $312 million. See NOTE 22, 'ACCELERA STRATEGIC REORGANIZATION ACTIONS,' to our Consolidated Financial Statements for additional information.
Total charges for these strategic reorganization actions were $312 million. [...] Segment EBITDA (764) [...] (312 million of charges in EBITDA).

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed across different segments. While the Power Systems and Distribution segments are experiencing strong growth, particularly in power generation and data center markets, the Engine segment is facing challenges in truck markets. Additionally, there is a weakening demand in global construction markets and North American heavy-duty truck and pick-up truck markets, which partially offsets the positive momentum in other areas.

evidence.text: The power generation market is growing, particularly in North America and Europe, while construction and heavy-duty truck markets are weakening.

Higher demand in power generation markets (mostly data center markets) and higher demand in North American medium-duty truck and bus markets were offset by the divestiture of Atmus, lower emission solutions demand (mainly in China), lower demand in North American heavy-duty truck and pick-up truck markets and weaker demand in global construction markets.
Power generation $ 3,985 [...]. [...] North American sales increased $544 million principally due to higher demand in power generation markets, especially data center and commercial markets.

factors.summary: The company's inventory levels are stable with slight increases, indicating normalizing trends, but there are strategic actions being taken to optimize stock, which resulted in inventory write-downs and impairments totaling $312 million.

evidence.text: Inventories $ 5,742   $ 5,677  [...] Inventory turnover 4.4   4.5

In the fourth quarter of 2024, our Accelera segment underwent a strategic review... As a result of these actions, we recorded several charges in the fourth quarter related to inventory write-downs, intangible and fixed asset impairments and joint venture impairments. Total charges for these strategic reorganization actions were $312 million.

factors.summary: Management exhibits cautious optimism, acknowledging challenges such as slower adoption of zero-emission solutions and geopolitical risks, particularly in high-risk countries like China and Brazil. They also face ongoing issues with input costs and inflation, which could negatively impact earnings.

evidence.text: As part of our growth strategy, we invest in businesses in certain countries that carry higher levels of these risks such as China, Brazil, India, Mexico and other countries in Europe, the Middle East and Africa. At the same time, our geographic diversity and broad product and service offerings have helped limit the impact from a drop in demand in any one industry, region, the economy of any single country or customer on our consolidated results.

Increases in costs, tariffs, as well as other inflationary pressures, could negatively impact earnings. [...] The potential for trade disruption, including embargoes, sanctions and export controls could negatively impact earnings.

factors.summary: The company is reorganizing its operations to focus on promising technologies, particularly electrified power systems and hydrogen production. While the long-term strategic direction is clear with investments in sustainable technologies, challenges remain in execution and market demand.

evidence.text: In the fourth quarter of 2024, our Accelera segment underwent a strategic review... This review resulted in decisions to consolidate certain manufacturing efforts, focus internal development efforts towards areas of differentiation while continuing to leverage partners and reduce our investments in certain technologies, joint ventures and markets.
We believe market demand for trucks in India will continue to be strong. [...] The company is investing in strategic reorganization actions to focus on promising zero emission solutions.

factors.summary: The company faces multiple key sensitivities including geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, regulatory compliance, commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign exchange movements. These factors could have significant impacts on both long-term operations and near-term financial results.

evidence.text: Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises... and regulatory matters... including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.
What variables (commodity prices, interest rates, volumes, FX, government budgets) does management identify as having the largest potential impact on near-term results?

factors.summary: The company faces a mix of near-term risks, including weaker demand for certain products, geopolitical instability, inflationary pressures, and potential trade disruptions, which could impact its financial performance.

evidence.text: Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve... Economic downturns in markets we serve generally result in reduced sales of our products and can result in price reductions in certain products and/or markets. As a worldwide business, our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises... and regulatory matters... At the same time, our geographic diversity and broad product and service offerings have helped limit the impact from a drop in demand in any one industry, region, the economy of any single country or customer on our consolidated results.

We expect demand for medium-duty and heavy-duty trucks in North America to remain relatively weak in the first half of 2025. [...] Increases in costs, tariffs, as well as other inflationary pressures, could negatively impact earnings. [...] The potential for trade disruption, including embargoes, sanctions and export controls could negatively impact earnings.

factors.summary: The company is consistently increasing its R&D investment, particularly focusing on emission solutions, new technologies, and innovative components such as battery and electric power systems. This strategic focus indicates a commitment to future growth and innovation.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products and improvements of current technologies to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas-powered engines and related components, as well as development activities around electrified power systems with innovative components and systems including battery and electric power technologies and hydrogen production technologies.

evidence.text: Construction and infrastructure spending is weakening, impacting demand for certain products.
